truecar.note: Body-style filter broken per spec — skipped.

top10.introHtml: Rankings favor low mileage, top condition, and fun-to-drive character over raw price. Reliable convertible families (Saturn Sky/Pontiac Solstice, BMW Z3, Mustang) rank highest; problematic models (Chrysler Crossfire, Saab, high-mileage European electrics) rank lower. Clean-title / no-accident filters applied on Cars.com listings. CarGurus listings lack location data — call ahead to confirm.

picks.whyGood: GM Kappa platform — same bones as Pontiac Solstice, RWD lightweight roadster. This one has only 39,382 miles for a fun sporty convertible under $13k, which is exceptional. Turbocharged Red Line version adds serious punch if this is the turbo trim.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate top through full cycle; check for headliner sag and water staining on rear seats/carpet; inspect convertible top latches and rear plastic window; Ecotec engine: check for any oil leaks, coolant condition; test A/C; check for rust on sills.
picks.bottomLine: Exceptional mileage for the money — Saturn Sky is an often-overlooked fun car; verify top condition and get a PPI.

picks.whyGood: BMW's lightweight E36-based roadster. At 75,828 miles and $9,990 it's a strong entry into rear-wheel-drive fun. Inline-6 models (2.3L, 2.5L, 2.8L, 3.0L) are preferred over the 4-cylinder. Straightforward mechanicals with wide parts availability.
picks.whatToVerify: Check rear soft top for tears and delamination, rear plastic window yellowing/cracking; top pump and latch operation; oil leaks common (valve cover gasket, oil pan drain plug area); check for cooling system maintenance (BMW plastic cooling components age); E36-spec Z3: inspect for subframe cracks at rear mounting points.
picks.bottomLine: Sub-$10k Z3 roadster is a strong buy if the top and cooling system check out; great entry into BMW fun.

picks.whyGood: Ford Mustang convertible — America's definitive affordable drop-top. 1994 model at 44,000 miles and $10,500 is solid value.
picks.whatToVerify: SN-95 / early S197: check convertible top-mount-to-rocker frame rust, rear seat water staining. Test T-3650 transmission by slow-shifting to 3rd (synchro test). 8.8 rear axle: listen for clunk on takeoff. Top: operate through full cycle, check rear window seam condition.
picks.bottomLine: Mustang convertible delivers V8 muscle theater on a budget; verify frame, transmission, and top before buying.

picks.whyGood: MINI Convertible — fun city drop-top, unique character, decent reliability. At 52,000 miles for $12,000 this is a solid deal.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate top through full cycle, check for water intrusion in rear seats; inspect MINI-specific items: electric top motor, latch strikers; check for oil leaks (common on older MINIs); verify timing chain service history.
picks.bottomLine: Fun MINI drop-top at low mileage; verify top mechanics and timing chain before committing.

picks.whyGood: BMW's lightweight E36-based roadster. At 82,690 miles and $8,499 it's a strong entry into rear-wheel-drive fun. Inline-6 models (2.3L, 2.5L, 2.8L, 3.0L) are preferred over the 4-cylinder. Straightforward mechanicals with wide parts availability.
picks.whatToVerify: Check rear soft top for tears and delamination, rear plastic window yellowing/cracking; top pump and latch operation; oil leaks common (valve cover gasket, oil pan drain plug area); check for cooling system maintenance (BMW plastic cooling components age); E36-spec Z3: inspect for subframe cracks at rear mounting points.
picks.bottomLine: Sub-$10k Z3 roadster is a strong buy if the top and cooling system check out; great entry into BMW fun.

picks.whyGood: Ford Mustang convertible — America's definitive affordable drop-top. 2013 model at 64,381 miles and $10,499 is solid value.
picks.whatToVerify: SN-95 / early S197: check convertible top-mount-to-rocker frame rust, rear seat water staining. Test T-3650 transmission by slow-shifting to 3rd (synchro test). 8.8 rear axle: listen for clunk on takeoff. Top: operate through full cycle, check rear window seam condition.
picks.bottomLine: Mustang convertible delivers V8 muscle theater on a budget; verify frame, transmission, and top before buying.

picks.whyGood: E46 BMW M3 convertible — the purist's choice, S54 straight-six, one of the great driver's cars. At 143,000 miles this needs a PPI but could be a gem.
picks.whatToVerify: E46 M3: get a pre-purchase inspection from an independent BMW specialist. S54 engine VANOS: listen for cold-start rattle (VANOS solenoids); subframe cracks at rear: inspect visually and with flashlight; rod bearings at high mileage — ask for service records; soft top: full cycle test, rear window condition.
picks.bottomLine: Dream car territory if the PPI comes back clean — S54 M3 convertible is rare under $15k.

mustangs.introHtml: Ford Mustang convertibles ranked by trim (GT > V6), manual preference, mileage, and proximity. SN-95 (1994–2004): check convertible top-mount-to-rocker rust. S197 (2005–2014): test T-3650 3rd-gear synchro, listen for 8.8 rear axle clunk, check rear-seat water staining.
